original_abstract: This study examines the implementation of generative artificial intelligence (GAI) tools in higher education, focusing on the University of Guayaquil. The objective was to identify the most commonly used tools by faculty and assess the benefits and challenges of their adoption. The methodology included a literature review and surveys administered to 334 faculty members across various departments, using a structured questionnaire covering demographic aspects, knowledge, usage, and perceptions of GAI integration in teaching. The analysis revealed that ChatGPT is the most widely used tool, although its adoption varies significantly among faculties. Despite advantages such as personalized materials and improved educational planning, key challenges were identified, including resistance to change and inadequate training. The study concludes that the integration of generative AI has great potential to enhance education but requires strategic planning, ongoing faculty training, and clear guidelines to ensure its ethical and pedagogical use.
humanized_abstract: The main objective of this study was to verify the application of generative artificial intelligence (GAI) tools in a higher education institution, in this case, the University of Guayaquil, as well as to know which tools are used the most by teachers, and in this way, to get an idea of the advantages and disadvantages of applying GAI in teaching. This research was based on a theoretical framework and on a survey applied to a sample of 334 teachers of different faculties, through an online questionnaire that identified the demographic characteristics of the professors, their level of knowledge about the tools, as well as their degree of application and their opinion on the use of GAI in the classroom. From the results of the research, it was possible to determine that ChatGPT is the most used application in the faculties; however, there is a big difference in its application, since each faculty has expressed in a different way the use of the application for the development of academic activities, since they have taken advantage of the application to generate learning materials of their own, facilitating the development of academic activities, in addition to the drawbacks that teachers have had in their application, among which mention the resistance to change in relation to the generation of texts and the absence of training in relation to the use of applications. As a conclusion, this study determined that the generative artificial intelligence that is applied in the academic field, in addition to numerous advantages, has many obstacles to be taken into account. Therefore, the application of these tools in educational institutions requires a planning based on the needs that teachers must meet in the development of the activities that students must carry out, the realization of training and the regulation of the appropriate use of these resources.
